title: Except for Podman V2.0,  which other release version will be also supported? such as: 1.9.x ？ 1.8.x ？
body: Except for Podman V2.0,  which other release version will be also supported? such as: 1.9.x ？ 1.8.x ？Are there any rules or documents for supported release version?

comments.body: There was a partial answer on https://podman.io/blogs/2020/08/01/deprecate-and-remove-varlink-notice.html

Generally speaking, we provide full support for the most recent version, and backports of security issues and major bugfixes to a few previous versions. At present, we're starting to spin the Podman v2.0 branch down to only critical backports as we prepare to begin cutting RCs for Podman 2.1.0

We are currently building our own packages for 1.9.3, since there are no "subprojects" available for podman:

https://build.opensuse.org/project/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable

It was recently added as a feature for cri-o, for users that wanted to stay on older releases (or on branches):

https://build.opensuse.org/project/subprojects/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable

----

For podman there are source branches and release binaries, but no packages.

https://github.com/containers/podman/branches

https://github.com/containers/podman/releases

That is, there is only one "podman" package so if you upgrade - you'll be on 2.0.5
